methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Trilobio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Roya Amini-Naieni and Maximilian Schommer that has raised $11M to date. The broader Biotech & Health sector comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics, though no private peers operate at sufficiently similar scale and stage to support direct median benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation triangulation technique here. Using total capital raised as a proxy for current value, the analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $28M. The public-comp anchoring method cannot be applied absent revenue disclosure, which is standard for private-stage companies. The peer-median method likewise cannot be applied; while 16 same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the three or more datapoints required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $23M to $32M, centered on $28M. This range reflects the inherent uncertainty of applying a single method to a capital-light seed company without disclosed revenue or comparable private-market transactions. Primary uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier to Trilobio's specific technology and market conditions, and the absence of revenue-based or transaction-based anchoring.
